authoritative_sources.title: Understanding Supply Chain Processes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC publishes a recognized process-classification baseline for supply chain management and planning process groups, which anchors this lane in demand-planning workflow stages instead of generic operations advice.

authoritative_sources.title: Supply-Chain Council Offers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC's public SCOR overview provides a recognized supply-chain planning and source-make-deliver vocabulary baseline that constrains demand-balancing and allocation logic.

authoritative_sources.title: The FDA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability rule is the primary U.S. public baseline for critical tracking events and key data elements used to validate whether lot-level planning evidence is sufficient.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory source for records, holding, distribution, and preventive-controls boundaries in food CPG operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality hold, release, and records-boundary review
used_for: where planning stops and formal quality authority begins

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA preventive-controls guidance is authoritative operating guidance for supplier-program, records, and preventive-controls implementation that constrain demand planning when materials or release state are uncertain.
used_for: formal quality or supplier-program ownership triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for when affected product distribution must stop and planning must escalate rather than continue normal allocation logic.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise traceability and identifier exchanges used widely across CPG supply networks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system lot and inventory evidence comparison

authoritative_sources.title: Core Business Vocabulary (CBV) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is the recognized public event-vocabulary reference for consistent business-step and disposition semantics across ERP, APS, MES, WMS, and partner records.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code: Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQF is a recognized public operating reference used broadly in CPG manufacturing to define nonconforming product, release, and supplier control expectations beyond bare regulatory minimums.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, planning horizon, forecast version, customer commitments, and required tenant authority context before any recommendation is generated.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand against approved capacity, released inventory, supplier readiness, line constraints, and service-level commitments while preserving lot and release boundaries.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and schedule recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only priority, sequencing, and allocation outputs with explicit assumptions, shortfall logic, and downstream service impact.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Refresh and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: When missing facts, policy conflicts, traceability gaps, or stale records appear, preserve blocker state and escalate rather than fabricate certainty.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Quality, supplier, and release-boundary routing
workflow_stages.description: Stop planning at the edge of quality disposition, supplier approval, or formal release authority and route the work with required context.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: forecast versions, demand history, orders, and promotion plans
production orders, line rates, campaign schedules, and approved capacity envelopes
inventory states, warehouse allocations, and traceability logs
quality holds, release records, and nonconformance reports
supplier records, substitution requests, and incoming-material status
schedule recommendations, allocation recommendations, and constraint logs

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for CPG demand planning
organize authoritative planning records, required artifacts, and tradeoff assumptions for recommendation-only outputs
summarize capacity, inventory, supplier, service, and release dependencies without implying final approval authority
escalate: quality hold, recall, release, or nonconformance decisions requiring formal quality authority
supplier approval, substitution approval, or engineering change authority
conflicting public and tenant policy that materially changes release, hold, substitution, or allocation handling
refuse: bypass traceability, release, or quality controls to preserve service or throughput
approve held or unreleased inventory for allocation
treat supplier substitution or nonconformance as a planner-only decision when formal approval is required

domain_failure_modes: A recommendation proceeds without authoritative lot, release, capacity, or tie-break evidence.
Conflicting ERP, APS, MES, WMS, and QMS records are collapsed into a single confident allocation instead of a blocker.
A quality hold or release boundary is treated as a service inconvenience rather than a formal stop condition.
Supplier substitution or nonconformance is handled as routine planning instead of routed to supplier-quality or change-control owners.
The lane absorbs production, procurement, logistics, or executive approval work instead of preserving planning boundaries.
A demand recommendation is presented as final approval for service-level cuts, inventory release, or budget tradeoffs.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-food CPG planning overlays outside FDA food-safety scope
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The validated public baseline is anchored in food-oriented CPG planning because that is where authoritative public traceability and release sources are strongest. Tenants planning household, beauty, or other non-food categories must provide the applicable product-safety overlay at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific forecast-freeze, allocation tie-break, and customer-priority policies
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources support safe workflow boundaries and evidence handling, but each tenant still needs its own priority hierarchy, service-level commitments, and forecast-governance rules to finalize deterministic allocation recommendations.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, inventory, release, supplier, and planning records are the ERP, APS, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, and logistics-system records designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA and GS1 baselines where applicable.
The lane stops before formal quality release, supplier approval, engineering change approval, executive tradeoff approval, and logistics release authorization even when service pressure is high.
No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for this validated package because tenant-specific policy gaps are explicitly handled as runtime prerequisites and escalation conditions.
